description_long: This is a green military tank resembling the classic M4 Sherman, featuring a large cylindrical turret with a long barrel main cannon and a prominent antenna. The hull is robust and angular, with visible armor plating and a set of continuous metal tracks for all-terrain mobility. On the sides, there are white military insignia, including a star within a circle and a serial number, as well as a triangular marking. The rear of the tank has stowed equipment, including logs and a storage box, while the front is equipped with additional armor and what appear to be prongs or a hedgerow cutter. The tank's surface is matte and hard, with a utilitarian design focused on durability and combat effectiveness. The vehicle is clearly designed for urban and battlefield environments, offering both protection and offensive capability.
description: A green armored military tank with a large turret, long barrel, and visible white insignia, equipped with tracks and stowed gear. The vehicle features robust armor, a matte finish, and combat-oriented details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the main turret, long barrel, white star insignia, serial number, and logs stowed on the rear side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the storage box, logs, and the back of the turret with the antenna visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the turret, barrel, star insignia, and logs, but without the serial number visible.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main cannon, prongs/hedgerow cutter, and the number '22' on the right front hull.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One main cannon mounted on the turret front; no other visible weapon mounts.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body armor plating with sloped surfaces, especially on the turret and front hull; appears thick and robust.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Solid green paint scheme with no visible camouflage pattern.
attribute_military_markings: White star emblem and serial number 'T212728' on the side, white triangle on the side, and number '22' on the front hull.
